practices.statement: Keep repository instructions as the durable baseline and add extra layers only when a repeated workflow or routing need clearly justifies them.
Prefer references to existing docs, tasks, or files over duplicating the same guidance across prompts, instructions, and skills.
Validate frontmatter fields, file placement, and tool names before judging content quality because broken metadata silently defeats good guidance.
When a workflow is global or cross-workspace, verify installers, runtime commands, and documentation together so claims match actual behavior.
Use a shared cache only as a bootstrap source; every serious audit still needs live research and revalidation before promoting conclusions.
